review_text: Incredibly dull and much too long.

Perfect mix of old and new
Expertly filmed but ultimately it’s a set of action sequences on search of a story.
Action packed, well written and directed, and with a stellar cast. This movie is most likely the second best one in the series next to fallout.

"Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One" is a thrilling cinematic masterpiece that delivers heart-pounding action, an engaging plot, and impeccable performances. Tom Cruise once again shines as Ethan Hunt, taking us on an adrenaline-fueled ride full of jaw-dropping stunts and espionage. The movie's intricate storyline keeps you guessing, and the supporting cast, including Rebecca Ferguson and Simon Pegg, adds depth to the characters. With breathtaking cinematography and a pulsating soundtrack, this installment sets the bar high for the Mission: Impossible franchise. It's a must-see for fans of action-packed blockbusters, leaving you eagerly awaiting Part Two!
Idiot me, I watched "The Final Reckoning" before this one, hence it didn't make any sense. I quite enjoyed this flick even though I'm not a MI fan. Gonna have to watch the sequel, again..
A fun movie in the theater but hated that they killed off Ilsa. She brought so much to these last couple films. I enjoyed the action and stunts as always.
It used to be that the IMF would solve one impossible mission per film… now there’s an impossible mission per frame. Too much impossible for me, my friends.
Same boring old save the world, different day!

There are some great action set pieces and classic tom cruise but it was just too long! The story is convoluted, confusing, farfetched, and just absurd. Even for MI, it’s too much. The last one should’ve been the end and now it’s just for money and for Tom cruise to set stunt records.

Truly horrible. An implausible plot. Action scenes that defy physics. Casting choices chock full of DEI. Rediculous lengthy dialog while the clock is ticking down to zero. I hope this is the last Mission Impossible because what will the next one be? On the moon or Mars?

Rating: 8.7/10 A thrilling ride with Ethan Hunt and his IMF team taking on a rogue AI. Highlights - Engaging Story: Well-balanced pacing with plenty of twists and turns. - Strong Performances: Tom Cruise shines, with great supporting cast. - Impressive Action: Masterful direction and breathtaking stunts. Criticisms - Predictable Plot: Some viewers may find it predictable at times. - Underdeveloped Characters: Supporting characters could have more depth. Verdict An exhilarating ride appealing to fans of action-packed spy films. Recommended for a thrilling cinematic experience.
The movies are built around death-defying showcases of suspense and bravado that grow increasingly more mind-boggling with each sequel.
Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning Part One is an epic, amazing spy thriller with beautifully shot locations, amazing stunts, set pieces, action, and great performances from its amazing cast.

I thought this was just fine. This may be a me issue but I'm finding it more difficult to suspend my disbelief with Tom Cruise as Ethan Hunt. Combine that with a ramping down of stakes from the previous films where the threat was ultimately 3 nukes, and I think we find ourselves at an impasse. I still enjoyed the film, but I'm hoping the eighth film is the last. This needs to end, everyone just looks tired.
Great action and really engaging probably my second favorite of the franchise.

The series drops off a lot with dead reckoning. The whole idea of the entity is ridiculous. They literally handcuff Ethan in this effectively limiting his movements. 3 stars for the jump but not with paying for.
Fun action, good stunts, definitely worth watching

Dead Reckoning takes the easy route for Ethan Hunt’s final threat with an AI bent on destroying humanity using nuclear missiles—a danger that the last James Bond had already offered us. While the film drags at times, with moments that could have easily been trimmed, it still delivers the kind of high-stakes sequences we love in Mission Impossible, such as the train and airport scenes. That said, one can’t help but wonder why Ethan, who has access to face-mask technology that prevents him from being identified, would choose to walk into such a heavily monitored airport without using one. Still, the movie keeps us engaged, and in the end, it’s a thoroughly entertaining ride.

My god what a waste of nearly three hours of my life. With some of the previous movies the action sequences saved the day, but clearly the cupboard was bare this time. Fighting on top of a train?? Really?! May as well have done a sword fight up a staircase, or maybe running in and out of doors like Scooby Doo. And Tom Cruise is starting to look weird, David Hasselhoff weird.
Not bad, but definitely not the best of the mission impossible series. Tom Cruise always puts on a great performance!

I can honestly say this movie is one of the worst I've seen it quite some time. The thought that hundreds of people worked their tails off to produce this dreck is really disappointing — for THEM. The writers, producers (lookin' at you, Tom), and director all share the blame for this. It's almost unwatchable; it's that bad. The chase scene — is there more than one? I'm only half-way through this overlong, nearly 3-hour movie — is pretty good but we've come to expect at least THAT is decent. The acting, particularly on the part of Tom Cruise, is way too internal. If I can't see it on your face, Tom, I can't read your mind through the screen. What I cannot believe is that both critics (whom I usually ignore) and the audience (whom I usually trust) are sucked in by this terrible script, lousy acting (still lookin' at you, Tom), and substandard direction on this self-parodical, over-produced mess of a movie. Hard pass. I'm not sure if I'll even watch the next one if it's more of the same.
I really liked this show! Tom Cruise always gives a great performance!
Amazing movie all around. This had me so excited for the next one after this movie came out. The motorcycle scene and driving were so good in getting your attention.

Tom cruise never ages he is like a vampire along with Brad Pitt. I can not believe Tom Cruise does all of his stunts. They are amazing especially the train scene with Hayley Atwell. I cannot wait until part two come out. Story line is semi-believable. With the advancement of AI.
A great and fun MI movie.Not as good as some of the others, but one of the best in the franchise.

It's worth a watch if you're a fan of the series. Just don't expect your typical mission impossible, in terms of story that is. I love movies about AI and the potential dangers it brings to mankind, but i also aknowledge that incorporating that into this kind of movie will always be complicated and difficult. For that reason, the script here didn't do a great job. It had some cool scenes, but it wasn't enough for me. I'm still giving it a positive review, but this is not the best mission impossible had to offer.

I can’t understand the rating given on here for this movie. While I’m usually in for a far fetched romp this far fetched tale riding on the rise of AI is very hard to follow and understand. The Action is mediocre for the whole save the world genre. The dialog is the most unrealistic I’ve heard out side of children’s cartoons. If I hadn’t had a house full of guests invited to watch I would have save 3 hrs of my life for something more productive.

I liked this movie! It was not the best of all the Mission Impossible movies but it was good!
Nice and very well written ! Good balance
amazing and so nice . Tom cruise is really a legend

Such a disappointment! The film was so outrageously silly! I am a fan of the movies but to me, this was just ridiculous! I was hoping as the film went on it would get better, but the disappointment continued. One forgettable movie I’m sad to say
Let's face it, the best Mission Impossible is still the brilliant first movie, by a mile.
Tom Cruise's franchise doesn't disappoint.
Falls in line with all the other Mission Impossibles! Good overall!

I still think Fallout is the best Mission Impossible movie but this movie is pretty good
Definitely the best entry in the later half of the franchise. Characters, music, action is superbly done.

first english, below spanish. english: reckoning with 'mission: impossible'. the opening of 'mission impossible: dead reckoning' lacks the immediate impact that distinguished its predecessors; however, it soon reveals itself as a necessary prelude to one of the most impressive sequences in contemporary action cinema. the extended airport set piece, a calculated play of tensions and rhythms that shift with surgical precision, stands as a true lesson in cinematic suspense, able to sustain interest and heighten expectation with every passing minute. christopher mcquarrie, faithful to his style, once again delivers what he had already consolidated in previous installments, but with the virtue of reinventing himself each time. far from falling into fatigue or mechanical repetition, his narrative always finds a different angle, ensuring that long sequences, instead of exhausting, progressively build toward a climax of growing intensity. the plot, one of the most complex and best executed in the franchise, confirms the ambition of the project. although it does not aspire to be the most transcendent narration in the history of cinema, it firmly stands as a story that far surpasses the minimal line of argument usually demanded of the action genre. here, the saga makes clear —with a strong and confident voice— that mission impossible is not reduced to gunfire, explosions, and spectacular chases, but inhabits a space where narrative tension and structural intelligence are as much protagonists as the action itself. this chapter gathers echoes and parallels from previous entries, but does so with the maturity of a work that understands its own legacy and chooses to elevate it even further. the ending, vibrant and sustained, keeps both the audience and the characters themselves on edge, trapped in a final sequence that allows no respite and inevitably compels the viewer to wonder what will happen in the very next moment. spanish: sentencia a 'misión imposible'. el arranque de 'misión imposible: sentencia mortal' carece del impacto inmediato que distinguió a sus predecesoras; sin embargo, pronto se revela como un preludio necesario para una de las secuencias más impresionantes del cine de acción contemporáneo. la extensa puesta en escena del aeropuerto, un juego calculado de tensiones y ritmos que oscilan con precisión quirúrgica, se erige como una auténtica lección de suspense cinematográfico, capaz de sostener el interés y acrecentar la expectativa con cada minuto. christopher mcquarrie, fiel a su estilo, vuelve a ofrecer lo que ya había consolidado en entregas anteriores, pero con la virtud de reinventarse en cada ocasión. lejos de caer en la fatiga o en la repetición mecánica, su narrativa encuentra siempre un ángulo distinto, logrando que las largas secuencias, en lugar de agotar, construyan de forma progresiva un clímax de intensidad creciente. la trama, una de las más complejas y mejor ejecutadas de la franquicia, confirma la ambición del proyecto. aunque no pretende situarse como la narración más trascendental de la historia del cine, sí se impone como un relato que trasciende con creces la mínima línea argumental exigida al género de acción. aquí, la saga demuestra —con voz clara y firme— que misión imposible no se reduce a disparos, explosiones y persecuciones espectaculares, sino que habita en un terreno donde la tensión narrativa y la inteligencia estructural son tan protagonistas como sus propias escenas de riesgo. este capítulo recoge ecos y paralelismos de las entregas anteriores, pero lo hace con la madurez de una obra que entiende su propia herencia y decide escalarla aún más. el desenlace, vibrante y sostenido, mantiene tanto al espectador como a los propios personajes contra las cuerdas, atrapados en una secuencia final que no concede tregua y que invita, de manera inevitable, a preguntarse qué sucederá en el instante siguiente.

Mission: Impossible - Dead Reckoning impressed with its exhilarating action sequences and jaw-dropping stunts, which kept me fully engaged. However, the storyline seemed to lose momentum at times, feeling a bit sluggish. On the other hand, the cinematography, production quality, and soundtrack were top-notch, elevating the entire experience.

Very hard to me to give 5 stars because for me it really have to touch my heart, but in my opinion i like the movie and story behind it, bring history from the past and combined with the great actors and the stunts is really entertaining and worth the watch.
Not my favorite Mission Impossible but was still not bad.
